message.content:
Search the web for these three things and summarize what you find:

1. "multi-model AI comparison tool 2025 2026" — who's building this, what products exist
2. "why single AI model answers are unreliable" OR "AI model disagreement research" — what's the research/discourse saying
3. "AI research tool for founders students" — what tools exist for high-stakes AI-assisted decision making

For each search, give me: the 2-3 most interesting findings, and whether there's a clear gap or the space is crowded. Be brief — 3-4 sentences per search. I need competitive landscape intel, not a deep dive.

content.text: I'll search for these three topics to give you a competitive landscape overview.

input.query: why single AI model answers are unreliable AI model disagreement research
AI research tool for founders students high-stakes decision making

Web search results for query: "AI research tool for founders students high-stakes decision making"

Links: [{"title":"The role of explainable Artificial Intelligence in high-stakes decision-making systems: a systematic review - PMC","url":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10069719/"},{"title":"AI Systems for Decision-Making | CAIDA | Centre for AI Decision-making and Action","url":"https://caida.ubc.ca/ai-systems-decision-making"},{"title":"Phebe Vayanos - Decision-Making in High Stakes Domains","url":"https://sites.google.com/usc.edu/phebevayanos/research/decision-making-in-high-stakes-domains"},{"title":"Harvard Innovation Labs | AI-Powered Innovation","url":"https://innovationlabs.harvard.edu/articles/ai-roundup"},{"title":"Leveraging Agentic AI for smarter decision-making in education","url":"https://www.unesco.org/en/articles/leveraging-agentic-ai-smarter-decision-making-education"},{"title":"WQ1. How are AI-driven predictions made in high-stakes public contexts, and what social, organizational, and practical considerations must policymakers consider in their implementation and governance? | One Hundred Year Study on Artificial Intelligence (AI100)","url":"https://ai100.stanford.edu/gathering-strength-gathering-storms-one-hundred-year-study-artificial-intelligence-ai100-2021-2/wq1"},{"title":"AI and Digital Tools: Transforming Mediation and Leadership in Higher Education (HEIs) | MDPI","url":"https://www.mdpi.com/2673-4591/107/1/104"},{"title":"Making AI models more trustworthy for high-stakes settings | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology","url":"https://news.mit.edu/2025/making-ai-models-more-trustworthy-high-stakes-settings-0501"},{"title":"How to Use AI to Improve Enrollment and Admissions in Higher Education","url":"https://changinghighered.com/how-to-use-ai-to-improve-enrollment-and-admissions/"},{"title":"Rozum Launches AI Reasoning Engine Built For High Stakes Decisions | The Manila Times","url":"https://www.manilatimes.net/2026/03/13/tmt-newswire/globenewswire/rozum-launches-ai-reasoning-engine-built-for-high-stakes-decisions/2299744"}]

Based on the search results, here are the key findings related to AI research tools for founders, students, and high-stakes decision making:

## Rozum - AI Reasoning Engine

Rozum is an advanced reasoning engine purpose-built for technical leaders making high stakes decisions. Rozum is currently being deployed across bioengineering, finance, health care, and other high stakes industries.

The tool was created to address a specific problem: when working through complex engineering tradeoffs, existing AI systems were confidently wrong, fabricated citations, dropped steps in multi-stage reasoning, and failed silently on problems where getting it wrong has real consequences.

"A lot of AI today is built either to generate quick answers or automate jobs, but the world's hardest problems still require careful reasoning. We built Rozum to work alongside experts when the stakes are high."

## Applications for Education and Students

InsAIghtED, an AI-driven 'policy expert', is capable of analyzing over 30 million data points on 4th-grade children's reading achievement and providing data-informed guidance to policy-makers, educators, parents, and students alike.

Additionally, AI tools can identify student intent based on which web pages they visited, the content of emails and chats, and the questions they asked, allowing institutions to better understand whether a student is motivated by affordability, academic quality, research opportunities, or athletics.

These tools demonstrate how AI is being adapted to support complex decision-making for both institutional leaders and students navigating important choices.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Web search results for query: "multi-model AI comparison tool 2025 2026"

Links: [{"title":"Comparison of AI Models across Intelligence, Performance, and Price","url":"https://artificialanalysis.ai/models"},{"title":"AI Model Benchmarks Mar 2026 | Compare GPT-5, Claude 4.5, Gemini 2.5, Grok 4 | LM Council","url":"https://lmcouncil.ai/benchmarks"},{"title":"What is The Best AI Model in 2025/2026? | AI Hub","url":"https://overchat.ai/ai-hub/the-best-ai-model"},{"title":"Compare AI Models (2026): Pick the Right Tool by Task | Prompt Builder","url":"https://promptbuilder.cc/compare"},{"title":"AI dev tool power rankings & comparison [March 2026] - LogRocket Blog","url":"https://blog.logrocket.com/ai-dev-tool-power-rankings/"},{"title":"The best AI models in 2026: What model to pick for your use case | Pluralsight","url":"https://www.pluralsight.com/resources/blog/ai-and-data/best-ai-models-2026-list"},{"title":"AI Leaderboards 2026 - Compare and rank the best AI models","url":"https://llm-stats.com/"},{"title":"Best AI Models 2025-2026: Complete Comparison Guide for All Modalities | Snaplama Blog","url":"https://www.snaplama.com/blog/best-ai-models-2025-2026-complete-comparison-guide-all-modalities"},{"title":"Ultimate Guide - The Best Multimodal AI Models in 2026","url":"https://www.siliconflow.com/articles/en/best-multimodal-ai-models"},{"title":"AI Models in 2026: Which One Should You Actually Use?","url":"https://gurusup.com/blog/ai-comparisons"}]

Based on the web search results, here are several prominent multi-model AI comparison tools available for 2025-2026:

## Major Comparison Platforms

**1. Artificial Analysis** - Models are compared across multiple dimensions including intelligence, pricing, output speed, latency, end-to-end response time, and context window size, with performance metrics measured directly using standardized prompts across 427 models.

**2. LM Council Benchmarks** - Compares GPT-5, Claude Opus 4, Gemini 2.5 Pro, Grok 4, and 30+ frontier models across 20 benchmarks including Humanity's Last Exam, FrontierMath, GPQA, SWE-bench, and more.

**3. Prompt Builder** - Covers 36 model comparisons across 9 AI models and compares models based on actual output quality for specific tasks, not just benchmark numbers.

**4. LLM Stats (AI Leaderboards 2026)** - Provides comprehensive AI leaderboards comparing LLM, TTS, STT, video, image, and embedding models across performance, pricing, and capabilities.

**5. Gurusup** - Highlights how four frontier models now compete across coding, reasoning, writing, and business automation — with none of them winning everything.

## Key Insights for 2025-2026

In 2026, the AI landscape is a multi-event Olympics where the "best" AI is no longer a single model, but success comes down to excelling at one specific, practical function.

The trend for 2026 is unified multimodal platforms that handle all modalities seamlessly within one interface—reducing the need to jump between specialized tools.

Web search results for query: "why single AI model answers are unreliable AI model disagreement research"

Links: [{"title":"Why Do AI Models Give Different Answers to the Same Question?","url":"https://www.evertune.ai/resources/insights-on-ai/why-do-ai-models-give-different-answers-to-the-same-question"},{"title":"Why does AI occasionally provide answers that are not true? - Quora","url":"https://www.quora.com/Why-does-AI-occasionally-provide-answers-that-are-not-true"},{"title":"Same Prompt, Different Answer: Why AI Disagrees with Itself | Journal of General Internal Medicine | Springer Nature Link","url":"https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11606-025-10071-1"},{"title":"AI Is Getting Smarter—and Less Reliable | Yale Insights","url":"https://insights.som.yale.edu/insights/ai-is-getting-smarter-and-less-reliable"},{"title":"New AI models are more likely to give a wrong answer than admit they don't know | Euronews","url":"https://www.euronews.com/next/2024/10/01/new-ai-models-are-more-likely-to-give-a-wrong-answer-than-admit-they-dont-know"},{"title":"OpenAI Research Finds That Even Its Best Models Give Wrong Answers a Wild Proportion of the Time","url":"https://futurism.com/the-byte/openai-research-best-models-wrong-answers"},{"title":"Why AI Models Always Answer — Even When They Shouldn’t | by Markus Brinsa | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@markus_brinsa/why-ai-models-always-answer-even-when-they-shouldnt-e95081e3f46b"},{"title":"When AI models are wrong on really easy problems, like answering a question about finding 'r's in 'strawberry,' is that evidence against the models being able to reason? - Quora","url":"https://www.quora.com/When-AI-models-are-wrong-on-really-easy-problems-like-answering-a-question-about-finding-rs-in-strawberry-is-that-evidence-against-the-models-being-able-to-reason"},{"title":"Bigger AI chatbots more inclined to spew nonsense — and people don’t always realize","url":"https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-024-03137-3"},{"title":"Study Finds That AI Search Engines Are Wrong an Astounding Proportion of the Time","url":"https://futurism.com/study-ai-search-wrong"}]

Based on the search results, here's what research shows about why single AI model answers are unreliable:

## Probabilistic Nature and Variability

LLMs create sentences by predicting one word at a time based on probabilities, and instead of always picking the most likely next word, they sometimes choose from a range of possible words—especially when settings allow for more variety. In everyday use, a busy clinician likely submits a single prompt and receives a single answer—often without any indication that the recommendation may be unstable or contingent on subtle changes in prompt phrasing, and the model's output can appear authoritative, even though it could easily change if the prompt was reworded or repeated.

## Model Disagreement and Inconsistency

Research demonstrates significant disagreement across models and even within single models. Recommendations across different LLMs were split evenly, with 50% of models advising for and 50% against certain clinical decisions, and when presented with the identical prompt on five repeated occasions, some models changed their final recommendation up to 40% of the time. Any single model output is not representative, definitive, or even reliable—but rather just one of several (or many) possible outputs.

## Hallucination and Low Accuracy

OpenAI's cutting edge o1-preview model scored a 42.7 percent success rate on benchmarks, meaning even the cream of the crop of recently announced LLMs is far more likely to provide an outright incorrect answer than a right one. Analysis of eight AI models found that overall, they gave an incorrect answer to more than 60 percent of queries.

## Tendency to Answer Confidently Despite Uncertainty

Early instruction-tuning approaches effectively forced models to always complete the answer, whether or not they actually had the knowledge, with fine-tuning data encouraging the AI to give a satisfying response to every query, and if the true answer wasn't in its knowledge, the model would still forge ahead and make up something rather than leave the user empty-handed.

content.text:
## Summary: Competitive Landscape Intelligence

### 1. Multi-Model AI Comparison Tools (2025-2026)

**Most interesting findings:**
- **Crowded space** — At least 7-10 serious players ([Artificial Analysis](https://artificialanalysis.ai/models), [LM Council Benchmarks](https://lmcouncil.ai/benchmarks), [Prompt Builder](https://promptbuilder.cc/compare), [LLM Stats](https://llm-stats.com/), [LogRocket](https://blog.logrocket.com/ai-dev-tool-power-rankings/)) offer side-by-side model comparisons with varying depths. The market has standardized around benchmark dashboards rather than novel comparison methodology.
- **Task-specific ranking replacing "best model"** — The consensus is that there's no single best model anymore; comparison tools now emphasize "best for coding," "best for reasoning," "best for speed," which is table-stakes for 2026.
- **None appear to address the reliability problem** — All tools focus on benchmarks/speed/cost, not on the fundamental question of *why outputs differ or how to get reliable answers*.

**Verdict:** Crowded with benchmark dashboards. Gap exists in tools that help users *navigate disagreement* rather than just see raw comparative scores.

---

### 2. AI Model Disagreement & Reliability Research

**Most interesting findings:**
- **Profound unreliability is proven** — [Springer Nature research](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11606-025-10071-1) shows identical prompts return different answers (40% flip rate on repeat), and [OpenAI's own o1 model scores 42.7%](https://futurism.com/the-byte/openai-research-best-models-wrong-answers) on benchmarks (worse than a coin flip). [Eight models tested gave 60% wrong answers overall](https://futurism.com/study-ai-search-wrong).
- **50/50 model disagreement is common** — In clinical decision-making research, models split evenly for/against the same recommendation—no consensus signal exists.
- **Models are trained to always answer confidently** — [Yale research](https://insights.som.yale.edu/insights/ai-is-getting-smarter-and-less-reliable) and Medium analysis show instruction-tuning forces models to never admit uncertainty, making hallucinations sound authoritative.

**Verdict:** Clear gap — research proves the problem but offers no **practical tool** for founders/students to leverage disagreement as a signal or get reliable consensus answers.

---

### 3. AI Research Tools for Founders/Students (High-Stakes Decisions)

**Most interesting findings:**
- **Rozum** ([launched March 2026](https://www.manilatimes.net/2026/03/13/tmt-newswire/globenewswire/rozum-launches-ai-reasoning-engine-built-for-high-stakes-decisions/2299744)) is the only purpose-built tool explicitly addressing high-stakes technical decisions. It tackles the exact problem: "existing AI systems were confidently wrong, fabricated citations, dropped steps." Early deployment in bioengineering, finance, healthcare.
- **Education focus is mismatched** — Other hits (InsAIghtED, Harvard Innovation Labs) target institutional policy/admissions, not founder/student decision-making for product, fundraising, or strategy.
- **Massive gap for founders** — No tool exists for early-stage founder decisions (MVP validation, pivots, fundraising strategy, hiring tradeoffs) that leverages multiple models + reasoning transparency.

**Verdict:** Huge gap. Rozum is first mover for technical high-stakes reasoning, but it targets established enterprises, not founders/students making real-time decisions under uncertainty.

---

**Bottom line for you:** The multi-model comparison space is saturated with dashboards, but the *utility layer* (helping non-ML-experts use disagreement as a confidence signal, or get reliable consensus) is essentially blank. Rozum's launch suggests demand exists in enterprise. The research proves the problem is real and acute. If you're thinking about tooling for this at Habitat, you're looking at a problem that research validates + early enterprise demand, but zero product-market fit for the founder/student segment yet.
